DESIGNX
Complete Design Review · Feb 2026
Brand Strategy

POSITIONING &
PERSONALITY

The strategic foundation that every design decision is built on. DesignX is the professional home for design leaders building with AI.

Core Position
Professional home for design leaders building with AI — where senior practitioners learn what's working, share what they're shipping, stay ahead of a discipline changing faster than any one person can track.
8-year-old community that needs to feel next-generation. Not ADPList (mentorship), not Config (vendor conference), not Dribbble (portfolios). Closest to Lenny's/Reforge but community-first not content-first.
Target Audience
Primary
Senior IC → VP of Design
8-20 YOE at Shopify, Mastercard, NYT, Uber, Google. They're leading teams through AI transformation and need a peer group.
Secondary
Ambitious Mid-Level
5-8 YOE. Aspiring to lead. Want access to how senior designers think about AI, careers, compensation.
Commercial
Sponsors & Partners
Design tool companies, hiring teams, conference sponsors. They want access to 750 vetted design leaders.
Brand Personality
Personality Spectrum
Creative vs Corporate65% creative
Exclusive vs Open40% exclusive
Serious vs Playful55% serious
Maximal vs Minimal72% maximal
Zeitgeist vs Timeless60% zeitgeist
Community-Led vs Top-Down58% community
Voice Principles
INSIDER NOT INFLUENCER
In the room, not performing. We share because we're doing the work, not for engagement.
DIRECT NOT BLUNT
Say what we mean without softening. Respect people's intelligence.
GENEROUS NOT NEEDY
Share knowledge freely. Don't gate-keep or create artificial scarcity.
BUILDER ENERGY
Ships fast, experiments publicly. Show the work, not just the results.
Brand Architecture
designx.community
Marketing & content hub. Framer site. Talks, salary data, newsletter, events.
designleadership.io
DLS conference brand. Purple gradient world. Toronto + Austin 2026.
app.designx.community
Platform — tools, The Stack, job board. Next.js + Supabase. More functional UI.
Why Each Design Choice
Dark ModeCounter-positions vs all competitors. Enables chrome + neon. Signals "tool" not "blog".
Bold Uppercase TypeDesignX makes bold claims. Typography must match. PP Mondwest = digital craft.
Chrome 3D ObjectsVisual language of AI/frontier tech. AI-generated = fast, infinitely variable.
Color-Per-ContentMulti-product platform. Each product needs identity within system without separate logos.
Halftone PhotographyMakes phone photos intentional. Connects to PP Mondwest bitmap aesthetic.
Seasonal ModelBuilt-in scarcity + renewal moments + freshness. The "Season '26" framework.
Design Tokens

COLORS, SPACE,
MOTION

Every value rationalized against the positioning strategy. 3-tier backgrounds, 4-level text hierarchy, product-owned color worlds, motion tokens.

Backgrounds — 3 Tiers + Glass
Stage · #000000
Page background. The void.
Canvas · #0A0A0F
Cards, content areas. One step up.
Raised · #111118
Modals, dropdowns, tooltips.
Glass 1 · 3%
Glass 2 · 5%
Glass 3 · 8%
Glass 4 · 12%
Product Color Worlds
DLS / Events
#2D1B69→#A78BFA
The premium world
Salary
#0A2E0A→#AAFF00
Money talks
Jobs
#1E3A5F→#06B6D4
Opportunity space
Community
#78350F→#FCD34D
The human side
Season (Flex)
Multi-stop
Changes yearly
V2 → V3 Changes
Jobs got own blue worldWas sharing season gradient → now #3B82F6→#06B6D4
Red removed as content colorReserved for errors/destructive only
Backgrounds 8→3 tiers + glassEliminated decision fatigue
Text hierarchy 6→4 levelsink(100%) · ink2(70%) · ink3(40%) · ink4(18%)
Text Hierarchy
Ink · 100%
Headlines, primary content
Ink 2 · 70%
Body text, descriptions
Ink 3 · 40%
Supporting, metadata
Ink 4 · 18%
Disabled, decorative
Motion Tokens
Ease Out · 300ms
cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)
Page reveals, card entrances, dropdowns. The default.
Spring · 300ms
cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)
Button press, toggle, like/save feedback.
Drift · 3000ms
ease-in-out infinite
Chrome float, glow pulse, background movement.
Spacing Grid · 4px Base
4 · 8 · 12 · 16 · 20 · 24 · 32 · 40 · 48 · 64 — every margin, padding, gap is a multiple of 4
Border Radii — Only 3
r1 · 8px · small
r2 · 14px · cards/buttons
r3 · 20px · hero/large
Typography

TYPE SYSTEM
IN CONTEXT

Not specimens — type where it actually lives. DM Sans 800-900 for display, JetBrains Mono for data, Instrument Serif italic for emotional moments only.

Display — DM Sans 900
DLS '25 · Rapid Response
CLAUDE CODE
→ FIGMA
DM Sans 900 · 48px · -0.04em tracking · 0.88 line-height · uppercase always
Monospace — JetBrains Mono
Median
$175,000
P25
$142,000
P75
$210,000
Sample
n=127
JetBrains Mono 800 · Never DM Sans for numbers · Always mono for data
Serif — Instrument Serif Italic · 3 Legal Uses Only
DESIGN LEADERSHIP SUMMIT
Three days for the leaders shaping design's next chapter
Use 1: DLS tagline. The breath after the shout.
Design Lead
$175,000
Use 2: Salary role title only. Everything else = mono.
"The designers who'll thrive aren't learning AI — they're learning what to ask for."
Use 3: Pull quote. Newsletter/long-form. Max once per page.
⚠ Serif is NEVER for:
Body text, nav, buttons, labels, card titles, headers, social graphics, or anywhere in the app platform. If reaching for Instrument Serif, ask: is this an emotional moment or informational one?
Photography

DUOTONE &
BLEND MODES

Community photos through the brand pipeline. Grayscale → contrast → color overlay. Four blend modes create four completely different moods from the same color.

Before & After
Original — unprocessed
DLS 2025 CREW
Duotone Neon · multiply · 72%
Blend Mode Comparison — Violet on Networking Photo
Multiply
Rich, moody. Current default. Best for text overlays.
Overlay
Punchier. More dynamic range. Great for heroes.
Color Dodge
Glowy, ethereal. Use sparingly for accent moments.
Hard Light
Aggressive poster feel. The "bold claim" mode.
All Color Worlds — Speakers & Dinners
DLS Speaker
Deep Purple · #6D28D9
Designer Dinner
Coral · #E8927C
Featured Speaker
Electric Blue · #3B82F6
Stage Shot — Hero Banner Treatment
DLS 2022 · Keynote
LEADERSHIP LESSONS FROM THE APOLLO PROGRAM
Decision Rule: Photo vs Chrome
DUOTONE PHOTO → People moments
Event recaps, community spotlights, speaker headshots, social dinners, Instagram stories, newsletter community section.
CHROME 3D OBJECT → Content promotion
Talk/event promos, salary data cards, job board headers, The Stack tool cards, social thumbnails, landing page heroes.
Halftone System

THE DOT
PATTERN

Radial-gradient halftone layered on duotone. The "upgrade a phone photo to brand graphic" recipe. Pure CSS, swappable photos, production-ready.

Dot Size Comparison
DESIGN DINNERS
2px · Fine
Print-like. Subtle texture you feel more than see.
DESIGN DINNERS
4px · Medium ★
Sweet spot. "I notice this is designed." Recommended default.
DESIGN DINNERS
6px · Bold
Unmistakable halftone. Statement pieces and hero moments.

FIRST MEANINGFUL PAINT
Performance metrics for design teams
CSS Implementation
.ht img     { filter: grayscale(100%) contrast(1.4) brightness(1.05); }
.ht::after  { background: var(--duotone-color); mix-blend-mode: multiply; opacity: 0.85; }
.halftone   { background-image: radial-gradient(circle, #000 1.2px, transparent 1.2px);
               background-size: 4px 4px; opacity: 0.3; mix-blend-mode: overlay; }

UI ELEMENTS

Card Structural Rules
BackgroundbgCanvas (#0A0A0F). Never pure black.
Border1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.06). On hover: 0.12
Radiusr2 (14px) standard. r3 (20px) hero/full-bleed.
Padding16-20px body. 0px gradient headers.
HovertranslateY(-2px) + deeper shadow. 300ms ease-out.
Gradient areaProduct's gradient. Max 40% of card height.

WHAT'S LEFT

Open Decisions — Need Your Call
Visual Treatment Defaults
→ Is halftone the default for all community photos, or reserved for "graphic" moments while clean duotone handles cards?
→ Which dot size feels right as canon? (I recommend 4px)
→ Does each season year get a texture variation?
Brand Assets Still Needed
→ 3D chrome renders from Midjourney (sphere, ring, pill shapes)
→ PP Mondwest font files (for pixel "X" mark and accents)
→ Logo lockup finalized (DESIGNX in DM Sans 900 + X treatment)
High Priority — Build Next
Motion SystemTokens defined, need actual animations (page transitions, card entrances, chrome float)
Email TemplateNewsletter with duotone headers, mono data, card-based layout
Social Template SystemInstagram stories, LinkedIn posts, event announcements — templatized
Brand Sound / AudioNotification tones, talk intro sound, DLS opening
Medium Priority
Data Visualization SystemChart styles for salary data, community analytics
IconographyCustom icon set or curated from Lucide/Phosphor
Loading & Empty StatesSkeleton screens, error pages, zero-state illustrations
Presentation TemplateKeynote/Google Slides for DLS speakers
Merch & PhysicalHoodie design, badge design, sticker system, tote bags
Season Transition PlaybookHow visual identity shifts year to year within the system
